# Moda — Complete Domain Context for LLMs

This document provides comprehensive context about Moda's domain model, business rules, and architecture for AI agents and language models.

## System Overview

Moda is an intelligent delivery management platform designed to give engineering leaders and teams end-to-end visibility into software delivery. It acts as a unified hub that synchronizes data from multiple business systems and combines it with capabilities those systems lack — connecting the dots so teams can see the full picture in one place.

Built with .NET 10, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework Core, Next.js 16, React 19, and Ant Design. Follows Clean Architecture with Domain-Driven Design, organized as a modular monolith with a shared database.

### Ownership Model

Objects in Moda have an ownership attribute:
- **Owned** — Created and managed within Moda
- **Managed** — Synchronized from an external system (e.g., Azure DevOps) and read-only in Moda
- **System** — Moda system-owned, immutable by users

---

## Organization Domain

### Teams

Teams are the fundamental organizational unit. Properties:
- Name (up to 128 chars), Code (2-10 uppercase alphanumeric, unique), Description
- Active Date, Inactive Date (optional)
- Type: Team or TeamOfTeams
- Operating Model: Methodology (Scrum/Kanban) + Sizing Method (Story Points/Count)

Operating models have date ranges creating a history. New model auto-closes previous one. Teams must always have at least one operating model.

### Teams of Teams

Higher-level organizational unit containing child teams and other teams of teams. Provides rollup visibility across descendants. Same properties as Team but with child membership management.

### Team Memberships

Parent-child relationships with date ranges. States: Past, Active, Future.
Rules: One active parent per team, no overlapping date ranges, no circular references, both teams must be active.

### Deactivation Rules

Cannot deactivate a team if active memberships extend past the inactive date. Those memberships must end first.

---

## Planning Domain

### Planning Intervals (PIs)

Fixed timeframe (typically 8-12 weeks) for coordinated delivery. Properties:
- Name, Description, Date Range, ObjectivesLocked flag
- Contains: Iterations, Objectives, Team associations, Sprint mappings

States based on current date: Future, Active, Completed.

### Iterations

Time-boxed periods within a PI. Properties:
- Name (unique within PI), Category (Development / Innovation and Planning), Date Range
- Dates must fall within PI range and not overlap
- Can be auto-generated by week length via Initialize Iterations

PI Iteration detail pages aggregate metrics from all mapped team sprints (cross-team view).

### Sprints

Short time-boxed periods (typically 2 weeks) owned by teams. Properties:
- Name, Type (Sprint/Iteration), State (Future/Active/Completed), Date Range
- Team (optional), Ownership (Owned/Managed)
- Managed sprints have External Metadata (e.g., Azure DevOps iteration path)

Sprint Mapping: Each team's sprint maps to a PI iteration (one sprint per team per iteration). Removing a team from PI removes its sprint mappings.

### Sprint Metrics

| Metric | Formula |
|--------|---------|
| Total | Sum of all items/story points in sprint |
| Completed | Items where status category is Done OR Removed |
| In Progress | Items where status category is Active |
| Not Started | Items where status category is Proposed |
| Remaining | Total − Completed |
| Completion Rate | (Completed / Total) × 100 |
| Velocity | Completed count (story points or items) |
| Avg Cycle Time | Sum(Done − Activated) / Count for Done items with both timestamps |
| Missing Story Points | Count of items with null or zero story points |

"Completed" includes Done + Removed. Cycle time only calculated for Done items (not Removed) with both Activated and Done timestamps.

PI Iteration metrics: counts/points summed across sprints, cycle time averaged (simple, not weighted).

### Planning Interval Objectives

Team commitments within a PI. Properties:
- Team, Type (Team/TeamOfTeams), Status (NotStarted/InProgress/Completed/Canceled/Missed)
- IsStretch flag, Health Check (optional: Healthy/AtRisk/Unhealthy with expiration)
- Linked to work items that fulfill the objective

Business rules: Can only create when ObjectivesLocked is false. When locked, stretch flag is immutable.

### Predictability

Predictability = (Completed committed objectives / Total committed objectives) × 100
Stretch objectives excluded. Only calculated for Active/Completed PIs.

### Risks

Potential circumstances that could impact delivery. Properties:
- Summary, Description, Team, Reported By/On, Status (Open/Closed)
- Category (ROAM): Resolved, Owned, Accepted, Mitigated
- Impact (Low/Medium/High), Likelihood (Low/Medium/High)
- Exposure: Calculated from Impact + Likelihood (Low <4, Medium =4, High >4)
- Assignee, Follow-Up Date, Response, Closed Date (auto-set on close)

### Roadmaps

Visual timelines with hierarchical items. Properties:
- Name, Description, Date Range, Visibility (Public/Private), Managers (min 1)

Item types:
- Activity: Date range, can contain children (other activities, milestones, timeboxes), ordered
- Milestone: Single date point
- Timebox: Date range period

All items have optional Color. Roadmaps can be copied (deep copy preserving hierarchy).

### Planning Poker

Behind `planning-poker` feature flag. Real-time estimation via SignalR.

Estimation Scales: Named set of values (min 2). Configured in Settings.

Poker Sessions: Facilitator-managed, uses one scale (locked after rounds begin), immediately Active on creation.

Rounds: Voting → Revealed → Accepted. Can Reset (back to Voting). Votes only during Voting status.

---

## Work Management Domain

### Workspaces

Containers for work items. Properties:
- Key (2-20 uppercase alphanumeric), Name, Description
- Work Process (defines available types/workflows), Ownership (Owned/Managed)
- IsActive (inactive prevents adding/removing items)

Managed workspaces: Synced from external systems, have external URL templates.

### Work Items

Fundamental unit of work. Properties:
- Key: {WorkspaceKey}-{Number} (e.g., PLAT-1234). Title (immutable after creation).
- Type (WorkType), Status (WorkStatus), StatusCategory (Proposed/Active/Done/Removed)
- Priority, StackRank, StoryPoints, Assignee, Team, Parent
- Sprint/Iteration, Project (Portfolio tier only), ParentProject (inherited from parent)
- ActivatedTimestamp, DoneTimestamp, Created, LastModified

Supports: Parent/child hierarchy, dependencies, revisions, links, references.

Managed workspace items are read-only in Moda.

### Work Item Metrics (per item)

- Time to Start: Created → Activated (days)
- Cycle Time: Activated → Done (days). Formula: DoneTimestamp − ActivatedTimestamp
- Lead Time: Created → Done (days)

Portfolio tier items also show: Progress Pie Chart (child status distribution), Cumulative Flow Chart (daily status counts over time).

### Work Item Dependencies

Predecessor/successor relationships. Properties:
- Source (predecessor), Target (successor), Comment
- State: ToDo (source Proposed), InProgress (source Active), Done (source Done/Removed), Removed, Deleted
- Health: Healthy (predecessor before successor or Done), AtRisk (unplanned), Unhealthy (predecessor after successor or target active while source not done)
- Scope: IntraTeam or CrossTeam

Health calculated from sprint end dates (planned dates).

### Work Item Hierarchy Rules

- Only Portfolio tier types can be parents
- Parent must be higher level than child
- No self-links
- Portfolio tier items can be associated with PPM Projects; children inherit project

### Work Processes

Configuration linking work types to workflows. Properties:
- Name, Description, Ownership, IsActive
- Contains WorkProcessSchemes: WorkType ↔ Workflow mappings

Rules: Min 1 work type, each type once, only active processes assignable to workspaces.

### Work Types

Categories of work items. Properties: Name (immutable), Description, Level, IsActive.

Tiers:
- Portfolio: Container types, multiple hierarchy levels (Epic, Feature)
- Requirement: Base-level team work (User Story, Bug)
- Task: Child types (Task, Sub-task)
- Other: Non-standard, not in backlog views

### Work Type Levels

Default system levels: Epics (Portfolio L1), Features (Portfolio L2), Stories (Requirement L1), Tasks (Task L1), Other (Other L1). Custom Portfolio levels can be added.

### Workflows

Status progression definitions. Properties: Name, Description, Ownership, IsActive.
Contains WorkflowSchemes: WorkStatus + WorkStatusCategory + Order.

Owned workflow rules: Min 3 statuses, all 4 categories represented, each status once, categories grouped in order (Proposed → Active → Done → Removed).

### Work Statuses

Individual status values (e.g., "New", "In Progress", "Done"). Name is immutable. Reusable across workflows.

### Work Status Categories

Fixed normalization set:
1. Proposed — Not yet started
2. Active — Currently being performed
3. Done — Completed
4. Removed — Removed without completion

Enables cross-workflow metrics (cycle time, throughput, completion rates).

### Work Item Revisions

Audit trail: Revision number, who, when, field-level changes (old/new values).

---

## Project Portfolio Management Domain

### Portfolios

Top-level container. Properties:
- Name, Description, Status, Date Range, Roles

Status lifecycle: Proposed → Active (start date) → OnHold ↔ Active → Closed (all children closed, end date) → Archived (read-only).

Roles: Sponsor, Owner, Manager, Portfolio Manager, Stakeholder Manager, Governance Reviewer, Capacity Planner.

Contains: Programs, Projects, Strategic Initiatives.

### Programs

Groups related projects. Properties:
- Name, Description, Status (Proposed/Active/Completed/Cancelled), Date Range, Roles (Sponsor/Owner/Manager)
- Strategic Theme Tags

Rules: Only active programs accept projects. All projects must close before completing. Active requires date range. Only Proposed deletable.

### Projects

Most feature-rich entity. Properties:
- Key (ProjectKey, 2-20 uppercase alphanumeric), Name, Description, BusinessCase, ExpectedBenefits
- Status, Date Range, Expenditure Category, Lifecycle, Roles (Sponsor/Owner/Manager/Member)
- Strategic Theme Tags

Status lifecycle: Proposed → Approved (requires lifecycle) → Active (requires dates) → Completed/Cancelled.

Key can be changed (cascades to all task keys). Only Proposed deletable. At most one program.

### Project Lifecycles

Templates of phases. States: Proposed (editable) → Active (locked) → Archived.
Must have min 1 phase to activate. Only Proposed deletable/editable.

### Project Phases

Concrete instances from lifecycle template. Properties:
- Name (from template, not editable), Description (editable), Status (NotStarted/InProgress/Completed/Cancelled)
- Date Range, Progress (0-100%), Roles (Assignee)

### Project Tasks

Hierarchical work items within phases (WBS). Properties:
- Key: {ProjectKey}-{Number}. Name, Description, Type (Task/Milestone)
- Status (NotStarted/InProgress/Completed/Cancelled), Priority (Low/Medium/High/Critical)
- Progress (0-100 for tasks, 0 or 100 for milestones), Order, Dates, Effort (hours)
- Dependencies (Finish-to-Start, soft-deleted), Roles (Assignee)

Rules: Milestones cannot have children or be InProgress. Cannot delete tasks with children or active dependencies. No circular moves.

### Strategic Initiatives

High-level strategic efforts within portfolios. Properties:
- Name, Description, Status (Proposed/Approved/Active/OnHold/Completed/Cancelled)
- Date Range, Roles (Sponsor/Owner), KPIs, Projects

Rules: Only created in Active/OnHold portfolios. Deletable in Proposed/Approved. Completed/Cancelled = read-only.

### KPIs

Measurable indicators on strategic initiatives. Properties:
- Name, Description, StartingValue, TargetValue, ActualValue (from latest measurement)
- Prefix, Suffix, Direction (Increase/Decrease)
- Checkpoints: planned target values at specific dates with at-risk thresholds
- Measurements: actual recorded values over time

Checkpoint and measurement dates must be unique per KPI.

### Expenditure Categories

Project cost classification. Properties:
- Name, Description, State (Proposed/Active/Archived)
- IsCapitalizable, RequiresDepreciation, AccountingCode
- Settings frozen after activation (historical accuracy)

---

## Strategic Management Domain

### Visions

Organizational direction. Properties:
- Description, State (Proposed/Active/Archived), Dates (start/end timestamps)

Rules: Only ONE Active vision at any time. Archived visions cannot overlap in dates. Archived = immutable.

### Strategies

Approach for achieving vision. Properties:
- Name, Description, Status (Draft/Active/Completed/Archived), Date Range
- Independent of visions.

### Strategic Themes

High-level organizational priorities. Properties:
- Name, Description, State (Proposed/Active/Archived)

Rules: Only Proposed deletable. State changes publish domain events (PPM maintains synchronized copy).

Cross-domain usage: Tags on Projects, Programs, Strategic Initiatives for alignment tracking.

---

## Integration Domain

### Azure DevOps Boards Connector

One-way sync (Azure DevOps → Moda):
- Azure DevOps Project → Moda Workspace
- Azure DevOps Work Item → Moda Work Item
- Azure DevOps Work Item Type → Moda Work Type
- Azure DevOps Area → Team ↔ Work Item association
- Azure DevOps Iteration → Sprint / PI association

Requires PAT with Work Items Read scope. Each org linked by one connection. Background sync via Hangfire.

### Microsoft Graph

Syncs employees/users from Azure AD into Organization domain.

---

## Architecture Patterns

### CQRS with MediatR
All operations are commands (writes) or queries (reads) dispatched through MediatR. Controllers are thin.

### Result Pattern
Handlers return Result<T> from CSharpFunctionalExtensions. No exceptions for business logic.

### No Repository Pattern
EF Core DbContext used directly in handlers.

### Vertical Slices
Each service: Domain (entities, value objects) → Application (commands, queries, DTOs, validators) → shared Infrastructure.

### Feature Folders
Application layer organized by aggregate root: {Feature}/Commands/, {Feature}/Queries/, {Feature}/Dtos/.

### Time Handling
NodaTime types (Instant, LocalDate). Never DateTime.UtcNow — always inject IDateTimeProvider.

### Frontend Patterns
- NSwag-generated typed client for API calls (never authenticatedFetch directly)
- Ant Design theme tokens (never hardcode colors)
- Redux Toolkit + RTK Query for state/data
- CSS Modules with antd CSS variables preferred over theme.useToken()

---

## Key Relationships Summary

```
Organization
  Team → participates in → Planning Interval
  Team → owns → Sprint
  Team → commits → Objective
  Team → tracks → Risk
  Team → assigned to → Work Item

Planning
  Planning Interval → contains → Iteration
  Sprint → mapped to → Iteration (1 per team per iteration)
  Objective → linked to → Work Item
  Predictability = completed committed objectives / total committed objectives

Work Management
  Workspace → uses → Work Process → defines → Work Type ↔ Workflow
  Workspace → contains → Work Item
  Work Item → has → Status → belongs to → Status Category
  Work Item → depends on → Work Item (predecessor/successor with health)
  Work Item → parent of → Work Item (Portfolio tier only, higher level required)

PPM
  Portfolio → contains → Program → groups → Project
  Portfolio → contains → Strategic Initiative → tracks → KPI
  Project → has → Lifecycle → defines → Phase → contains → Task
  Strategic Theme → tags → Project, Program, Strategic Initiative

Strategic Management
  Vision (one Active) → guides → Strategy
  Strategic Theme → referenced by → PPM entities
```
